title: Local Networking, Name Resolution, and Secure Remote Access · System Lens: Interface Route Socket
summary: Restore access to a host by testing local configuration, route, name resolution, listening sockets, and SSH trust in causal order. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux interface route socket increment in a layered connectivity and SSH trust runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret interface addresses, routes, neighbors, and listening or established sockets as different network evidence.
Define restart limits, readiness checks, dependency order, failure escalation, and rollback evidence for a service change.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook

steps.title: Predict Interface Route Socket
steps.instruction:
theory case — Host has 192.0.2.40/24, default route 192.0.2.1, DNS 192.0.2.53, and service port 443. Name lookup succeeds while the socket is absent; test layers and preserve SSH host-key trust.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Interface Route Socket contract for the supplied data.
